rules.description: Checks for function docstrings that state that exceptions could be raised even though they are not directly raised in the function body.

def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises:
        ZeroDivisionError: Divided by zero.
    """
    return distance / time
rules.good:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.
    """
    return distance / time

rules.description: Checks for function docstrings that include parameters which are not in the function signature.
rules.bad:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.
        acceleration: Rate of change of speed.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.
    """
    return distance / time
rules.good:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.
    """
    return distance / time

rules.description: Checks for over-indented section underlines in docstrings.
rules.bad:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Parameters
        ----------
    distance : float
        Distance traveled.
    time : float
        Time spent traveling.

    Returns
          -------
    float
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises
      ------
    FasterThanLightError
        If speed is greater than the speed of light.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc
rules.good:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Parameters
    ----------
    distance : float
        Distance traveled.
    time : float
        Time spent traveling.

    Returns
    -------
    float
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises
    ------
    FasterThanLightError
        If speed is greater than the speed of light.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc

rules.description: Checks for undocumented public class definitions.
rules.good:
class Player:
    """A player in the game.

    Attributes
    ----------
    name : str
        The name of the player.
    points : int
        The number of points the player has.

    Methods
    -------
    add_points(points: int) -> None
        Add points to the player's score.
    """

    def __init__(self, name: str, points: int = 0) -> None:
        self.name: str = name
        self.points: int = points

    def add_points(self, points: int) -> None:
        self.points += points

def say_hello(n: int) -> None:
    """Says hello to the user.

    Args:
        n: Number of times to say hello.

    Yields:
        Doesn't yield anything.

rules.description: Checks for asyncio.create_task and asyncio.ensure_future calls that do not store a reference to the returned result.
rules.bad:
import asyncio

for i in range(10):
    # This creates a weak reference to the task, which may be garbage
    # collected at any time.
    asyncio.create_task(some_coro(param=i))
rules.good:
import asyncio

background_tasks = set()

for i in range(10):
    task = asyncio.create_task(some_coro(param=i))

    # Add task to the set. This creates a strong reference.
    background_tasks.add(task)

    # To prevent keeping references to finished tasks forever,
    # make each task remove its own reference from the set after
    # completion:
    task.add_done_callback(background_tasks.discard)

rules.description: Checks for the use of IOBase.write in a for loop.

rules.good:
from pathlib import Path

with Path("file").open("w") as f:
    f.writelines(lines)

with Path("file").open("wb") as f_b:
    f_b.writelines(line_b.encode() for line_b in lines_b)

rules.description: Checks for function docstrings that do not document all explicitly raised exceptions.
rules.bad:
class FasterThanLightError(ArithmeticError): ...


def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc
rules.good:
class FasterThanLightError(ArithmeticError): ...


def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises:
        FasterThanLightError: If speed is greater than the speed of light.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc

    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    ----------

        Distance traveled.
    time : float
        Time spent traveling.
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Speed as distance divided by time.
    Raises

    Args:

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises:
        FasterThanLightError: If speed is greater than the speed of light.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc
rules.good:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Args:
        distance: Distance traveled.
        time: Time spent traveling.

    Returns:
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises:
        FasterThanLightError: If speed is greater than the speed of light.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc

rules.description: Checks for uses of comparators other than < and >= for sys.version_info checks. All other comparators, such as >, <=, and ==, are banned.

>>> print(sys.version_info)

>>> print(sys.version_info == (3, 8))
False
>>> print(sys.version_info <= (3, 8))
False
